title: [lora_l2.h:95-97] rx callback signature exposes raw rssi/snr without range validation
description:
Completeness: API contract validation category.

The lichen_lora_rx_cb_t typedef at line 95-97 passes rssi (int16_t) and snr (int8_t) directly from lora_recv() to callbacks.

The Zephyr lora_recv() API does not document valid ranges for these values. Different radio drivers may return:
- rssi: typically -120 to 0 dBm, but some drivers return positive values or sentinels
- snr: typically -20 to +10 dB, but varies by driver

Callbacks that assume rssi < 0 (e.g., for display or threshold logic) may misbehave with non-conforming drivers.

The existing code in lichen_l2.c just logs these values, which is safe. However, the API exposes them to external callback implementers who may make assumptions.

Consider documenting expected ranges in the typedef comment, or adding validation/clamping before callback invocation.
close_reason: Fixed: documented expected rssi/snr ranges in callback typedef, noting values are driver-dependent
